name: Honda Accord V6
reliability: 9/10 — the J35 V6 is famously durable; the main asterisk is VCM-related oil consumption and motor-mount wear
cost_to_own: Insurance: Low (family sedan, low theft/claim profile) · Maintenance ~$450/yr
popular_mods: VCM disabler (VCMuzzler II / S-VCM controller) — the #1 'mod'; keeps all 6 cylinders active to cut oil burn and motor-mount wear
Cat-back exhaust for a deeper V6 note
Coupe V6 6MT is the enthusiast pick — shares J-series DNA with a deep tuning/swap community
oil_interval: Every 7,500 miles (or per the Maintenance Minder)

generations.9th Gen (2013–2017): The last V6 Accord. 278hp J35Y6 'Earth Dreams' direct-injected V6 in EX-L/Touring sedans and the EX-L/Touring coupe (6-speed manual available on the coupe). 2016 was the mid-cycle refresh: restyled front end, LED headlights, Apple CarPlay/Android Auto, and available Honda Sensing safety tech.
generations.V6 discontinued after 2017: The 10th-gen Accord (2018+) dropped the V6 entirely for turbocharged 4-cylinders, making the 2016–2017 V6 a sought-after 'last of its kind' for buyers who want a naturally-aspirated six.

common_issues: VCM (Variable Cylinder Management): cylinder deactivation can drive elevated oil consumption, spark-plug fouling, and premature engine/motor-mount wear (the rear mount especially)
Idle vibration as motor mounts age (often VCM-aggravated)
Direct-injection intake-valve carbon buildup at high mileage (milder than on turbo DI engines)
Early-build infotainment lag/quirks on some units
maintenance_tips: Run 0W-20 synthetic and don't stretch intervals — VCM engines are sensitive to oil level, so check it between changes
Many owners fit a VCM disabler to reduce oil burn and motor-mount wear
Service the 6-speed automatic with genuine Honda ATF DW-1 every ~30–50k miles
Inspect motor mounts if you notice new idle vibration
